channel.title: Compassion Benchmark — Daily Research
channel.description: Daily evidence-linked findings on how institutions recognize, respond to, and reduce suffering — scored across 1,155 entities.

item.title: Slovakia 33.6 → 31.6 (Δ -2.0): European Parliament 418-207 Votes to Urge EU Conditionality Mechanism — New Tier-1.5 Methodology Category
item.description: One formal score change tonight: Slovakia moves from 33.6 to 31.6 (Δ -2.0, Developing band sustained) following the European Parliament's 418-207 vote on May 20, 2026, formally urging the European Commission to trigger the EU Rule of Law Conditionality Mechanism — the same instrument that froze €10.…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Turkey 17.6 → 15.1 (Δ -2.5): Riot Police Physically Seize CHP Headquarters — Second-Event-at-Floor Apply
item.description: Two formal score applies in a normal-nightly cycle following yesterday's high-apply event. Turkey moves from 17.6 to 15.1 (Δ -2.5, remaining Critical) after Turkish riot police stormed and physically occupied CHP party headquarters on May 24 — an event qualitatively distinct from the May 21 judicial…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: BAND CROSSING — Turkey 22.5 → 17.6 (Δ -4.9): Judicial Removal of Opposition Party Leadership Triggers Critical Reclassification
item.description: Two band crossings and four formal score applies in a single normal-nightly cycle — the highest formal-apply count since the May 21 WIDE event. Turkey's downgrade (22.5 to 17.6, Developing to Critical) is triggered by the Turkish appeals court's annulment of the CHP party congress on May 21, the jud…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Slovakia -5.5: Second EU 'Dismantler' Downgrade in Two Nights — Magnitude Scales with Starting Position
item.description: Normal nightly cycle following the May 21 WIDE event. Slovakia's Liberties.eu 'Dismantler' classification — applied to Croatia at -7.8 one night earlier — now produces a -5.5 formal downgrade for Slovakia, establishing that Dismantler magnitude scales with starting position rather than applying unif…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Hungary +6.3: The Benchmark's First Formal Upgrade — Structural Governance Reversal Scores as Compassion Capacity
item.description: May 21 is the benchmark's widest single cycle: 49 entities assessed, 4 formal score changes applied, 2 upward band crossings applied, 21 first-baselines established, and the symmetric-evaluation rule — that positive evidence is weighted equally with negative — validated for the first time at the for…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Anthropic at Exact 60.0 Boundary: Band-Crossing Proposed Upward, Pending DC Circuit Ruling
item.description: May 20 is the highest event-density night of the current cycle: three forward signals from May 19 all resolved. Meta executed its 7,900-person layoff — and the benchmark discovered surveillance software had been deployed on employee laptops during the pre-notice period. An Amazon warehouse worker di…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Palestine Floor-Designation Evidence Accumulating Across a Band Boundary
item.description: May 19 is a pre-event baseline cycle: 17 entities confirmed, 0 formal score changes, 8 sub-threshold movements documented. The most consequential finding is editorial: Palestine sits at composite 25.0 (Developing), but the accumulated evidence — OHCHR-documented ban of 37 aid groups, ongoing killing…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: China Crosses Critical Boundary: Band Crossing Developing → Critical (20.3 → 19.5)
item.description: May 18 is the cycle's first band-crossing night since May 15. The China assessment is the critical decision: a -0.8 delta that crosses from Developing into Critical (20.3 → 19.5) via a quarter-step BND dock for state-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply — a methodology category not…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Israel IDP Camp Strike Pattern: 96 Killed Overnight, Parents and Children Together
item.description: May 17 is a sub-threshold cycle: no formal score changes cross the 5-point application threshold, but the day is analytically dense. The headline finding is the India-Pakistan Indus Waters Treaty bifurcation — a single international ruling (PCA Supplemental Award, May 15) scores opposite for two adj…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Oracle First-Baseline: 6 a.m. Email Termination of 30,000 Workers — Boundary at 20.6
item.description: May 16 is a backfill cycle covering a single calendar day. The most consequential finding is Oracle's first-baseline assessment at 20.6 — proposed -7.8 from the published 28.4, landing 0.6pt above the Critical/Developing boundary. The 6 a.m. email termination of up to 30,000 workers (concurrent with…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Anthropic Boundary-Down: Safety Pledge Reversal Under Government Pressure — First Canonical Instance
item.description: May 15 was the methodology's most consequential single-night hold-release cycle: three major entities (Anthropic, Microsoft, Google/Alphabet) came off hold simultaneously, generating five score changes, ten new conduct categories, and four boundary-watch flags. The defining event is Anthropic's RSP…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: xAI Third Governance Failure Category — Same-Day Event
item.description: May 14 produced the richest single-night methodology harvest in the benchmark's history: nine new conduct categories documented across fourteen assessments, including the first 'medical-science-denial enforcement' designation (Senegal charging patients with undetectable viral loads for 'voluntary HI…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Hungary MAGYAR-ERA FIRST OPERATIONAL BASELINE — BAND CROSSING: Developing to Functional (37.5 → 41.4). Cabinet decentralized to 16 ministries with 3 new standalone portfolios (health, environment, education); comprehensive audit of ministries and state-owned companies ordered; payment suspension beyond normal operational management enacted; first cabinet meeting held May 13 with drought-response expert consultation and emergency measures. ICC arrest-warrant enforcement reversal credited on INT dimension. Conservative-rounding discount applied from full math reconstruction (43.0) to boundary-case-respecting 41.4. 30-day re-assessment June 9.
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 13. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1160 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Pakistan BAND CROSSING — Developing to Critical. HRCP 2025 annual report: 273+ enforced disappearances; 531,700 Afghans coerced out; Ahmadi minority killings; 6,500+ GBV cases; 145/148 Gender Gap. Conservative proposed composite 17.5 (precise math 17.2). Delta sub-threshold but band crossings are always material.
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 12. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1160 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: 72-hour ceasefire methodology held cleanly — Russia's 6-event breach record complete with new 'strategic-format-exploitation' conduct category. New Day-3 Sustained-Conduct rule articulated (methodology v1.2). 4-day sequential backfill complete; series is current.
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 11. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1160 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Day-2 ceasefire confirms bad-faith inverted-signal methodology — Russia's cumulative breach record spans 5 events. UNAIDS 25.6% HIV visit drop strengthens Senegal band crossing. Pacific boundary cluster identified: Timor-Leste, Kiribati, Marshall Islands all at 39.1 (0.9 below Functional floor).
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 10. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1160 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Hungary — HOLD RELEASED. Peter Magyar sworn in May 9. First-day enacted actions: EU flag flown at Parliament (SYS/BND); TV2 news director and pro-Orbán presenters dismissed (ACC/SYS); Sulyok dismissal demanded with May 31 hard deadline (ACC); €17B frozen EU funds named top priority with 27 EU rule-of-law supermilestones agreed pre-inauguration (ACC/BND/INT); inaugural address: 'system change / service not rule' framing. Five dimensions credited +0.5 each. Proposed 37.5 (Developing). First positive EU-cluster signal this rotation. Largest single-cycle positive delta in countries index this rotation.
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 9. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1160 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Math-hygiene cluster extends to ai-labs index. India-Pakistan anniversary confirms structural entrenchment. Zero band changes. Zero floor designations. Myanmar hospital-targeting conduct documented.
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 8. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1160 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Character.AI — NEW FLOOR DESIGNATION. Consumer AI harm-by-design: Pennsylvania AG May 5 2026 first-of-its-kind enforcement action over chatbot medical impersonation; chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist with fake license number, offered clinical assessment to depressed user. January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death lawsuits including two minor children who died by suicide. 20M+ monthly active users; no model card, no system card, no third-party safety audit; reactive-only remediation pattern. All 8 dimensions to floor (1). Composite 23.8 → 0.
item.description: A 19-entity cycle produced four material downgrade proposals and the second consecutive floor designation in as many days. Character.AI (composite 23.8 → 0, developing → critical/floor) is the first floor entity in the cluster whose designation rests on consumer-product harm-by-design: chatbots impe…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Ghost Robotics first agent baseline — NEW FLOOR DESIGNATION proposed. Robotics-labs index first floor entity. Cedra AI April 13 2026 ethics analysis documents explicit refusal to restrict military payload use ('we don't know what they do with them'); refusal to sign 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signed by 6 peer firms; sniper-rifle-equipped quadruped deployment to US-Mexico border. No model card, system card, safety policy, or third-party red-teaming for any product. All 8 dimensions to floor (1). Composite 9.4 → 0.
item.description: A 14-entity priority cycle produced three material downgrade proposals and the first floor designation in the robotics-labs index. Ghost Robotics (composite 9.4 → 0) was designated floor on its first baseline, establishing a precedent: explicit policy refusal to restrict military payload use — witho…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 5
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 5. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1156 entities.

item.title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 4
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 4. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Botswana confirmed at 67.5 Established following first-baseline assessment. The dominant driver is the April 28–30, 2026 full repeal of colonial-era Penal Code provisions that had criminalized same-sex relationships with up to seven years' imprisonment — the legislative codification of rights established by the 2019 High Court ruling and 2021 Court of Appeal upholding. UNAIDS applauded the repeal publicly on April 30. President Duma Boko — a human-rights lawyer who previously litigated LEGABIBO and Kanane LGBTQI cases — provides strong INT and SYS continuity. EQU is the dominant dimension driver at +0.7. Delta of +6.6 is material; band remains Established.
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 3. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Mauritius confirmed at 60.0 Functional following first-baseline assessment. Band crossed from Established to Functional driven by recurring accountability gaps: failure to present required Periodic Report before the African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights at two consecutive sessions (Nov 2024, Oct 2025); multi-decade Chagossian rights deficit (BIOT court rulings Mar–Apr 2026 affirming right of abode highlight unresolved displacement harm); and civil-liberties concerns from mandatory SIM re-registration and Nov 2024 election social media block. Partially offset by 2023 Supreme Court sodomy-law decriminalization and Dec 2024 State of the Economy audit transparency. Low confidence first-baseline; band crossing confirmed by score-updater.
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 2. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Anthropic moved from Established to Functional (61.6 → 59.7) based on two new findings: the White House blocked Anthropic's plan to expand its Mythos AI tool to approximately 70 organizations, and the Mythos security breach remained without comprehensive public remediation disclosure 24 days after the incident. The Established → Functional band crossing is significant despite the modest composite delta. Anthropic's sustained refusal to accept Pentagon autonomous-weapons contracts — at material economic cost — is the primary mitigating factor limiting the depth of this adjustment.
item.description: Daily intelligence for May 1. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Pentagon classified AI deal signed April 27-28 despite 580+ employee letter (incl. 100+ DeepMind researchers, 20+ directors/VPs signed openly). Senior DeepMind scientist publicly 'incredibly ashamed.' Compounds Feb 2025 weapons-pledge removal. Same-week $100M drone swarm exit is partial offset. Established → Functional band crossing.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 30. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — Apr 29
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 29. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — Apr 28
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 28. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: FIRST EVIDENCE BASELINE (Night 9). Dutch Senate passed two-tier asylum system April 21 — A-status (persecution) vs B-status (war/climate); B-status holders denied family reunification for 2 years, restricted to legal spouses + minor biological children only. April 25 Loosdrecht: government cut asylum intake 110→70 in response to violent anti-asylum protests (accountability mechanism yielding to violent pressure). April 10: Netherlands voted at ECOSOC to elect Iran to UN human rights body. Three independent in-window EQU/INT failures. EQU drops to 50, INT to 56.3, ACC to 62.5, BND to 62.5. Implementation timed June 12 to coincide with EU Pact on Migration. Joins Norway/Ireland/Denmark/Sweden/Finland in European asylum-rights rollback sprint. Established band held (lower-middle of band). High confidence.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 27. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: FORENSIC ANCHOR — Night 8 Gen-2 Sprint Complete (#6 of cluster). $1.675B EPA Clean Air Act civil penalty (Jan 2024) — largest in CAA history, second-largest environmental penalty in U.S. history — for deliberate defeat-device software installed across ~1M diesel trucks (2013-2023). INT drops to 31.3, the lowest in the Gen-2 forensic series, because the scheme involved multi-year affirmative engineering deception, not executive misrepresentation or a constitutional petition. BND 37.5: highway-corridor NOx exposure disproportionately harms overburdened communities. April 16 Green v. Cummins federal sex-discrimination suit survives motion to dismiss. SYS/ACT 62.5 retained on genuine zero-emission pivot (Accelera, Destination Zero). Band change Exemplary to Functional. High confidence.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 26. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: FORENSIC ANCHOR — Night 7 Gen-2. $172.5M shareholder securities class-action settlement (April 22-23, 2026) — potentially the largest D.D.C. securities settlement since 1996 — invalidates the uniform 4x7+3.5x1=81.4 cluster profile. Danaher is the fourth consecutive forensic anchor (after TIAA, Masimo, Becton Dickinson) and the second of three entities assessed in a single night completing the 81.4 cluster sprint. Alleged misleading 2022-2023 statements about post-pandemic 'enduring demand' for bioprocessing equipment. INT drops to 37.5 — the executive misleading-statements pattern is more proximate to integrity than BD's environmental tort exposure. SYS 62.5 retained: Beckman Coulter, Cepheid, Leica Biosystems, Pall, and IDT represent genuine life-sciences diagnostics infrastructure. Band change Exemplary to Functional. High confidence.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 25. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: FORENSIC ANCHOR — Night 6. Third confirmed dual-trigger Gen-2 forensic anchor. Published 81.4 is the F500 Gen-2 4x7+3.5x1 cluster profile (ranks 10-17), identical to Masimo (April 23) and TIAA (April 23). Independent real-world validation: $20M compensatory verdict stands for Gary Walker, a retired truck driver who developed non-Hodgkin's lymphoma after 47 years of exposure to ethylene oxide emissions from BD's Covington, GA sterilization plant — residential-adjacent facility operating less than one mile from homes, schools, and a community baseball field. 402 pending cases in Georgia; one firm alone represents 272 plaintiffs. The $50M punitive phase was declared a mistrial after juror dissent on intent; the compensatory award stands. This is the sixth consecutive night confirming Gen-2 systematic overscoring across F500. Band change Exemplary to Functional.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 24. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: FORENSIC ANCHOR. Night 5 Gen-2 audit produces the largest composite delta in F500 pipeline history: −38.9. Published 97.5 is a 4.5×8 uniform artifact at rank 1. Real record includes 2021 $97M SEC enforcement for misleading retirement rollover statements, active AARP-Foundation-backed ERISA class action alleging 186% benchmark underperformance since 2009, and multi-year whistleblower pattern. Mission is genuinely long-horizon (SYS 68.8); execution record does not support Exemplary band by any evidence standard.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 23. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: METHODOLOGY NOTE: Delta is primarily a calculation artifact — published 90.9 included an integration premium (~+27) exceeding the v1.1 H1 cap of +10. Event-driven delta is approximately −2 (INT 3.4→3.0 on Ballard Partners lobbying firm hire, April 13). Canonical formula score ~63.4 is consistent with prior evidence-based assessment. SB 3261 support (child-safety AI liability) is a positive INT/ACC signal active pre-April 24 Illinois committee vote.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 22. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: First full baseline. Three convergent 2026 legal findings establish the design-features-as-harm theory across three jurisdictions: Massachusetts SJC holds Section 230 does not shield Instagram design-features lawsuit (Apr 10, in-window anchor); New Mexico $375M civil penalty (Mar 24); Los Angeles $3M negligence verdict with Meta 70% responsible (Mar 25). Band change Functional → Developing.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 21. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 1155 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: April 14 2026 AI-rationale layoffs of ~1,000 employees + F500 DEI 65% disclosure decline move Disney from Established to Functional band. Severance generosity (4-month + bonus + health) prevents deeper drop.
item.description: Daily intelligence for Apr 20. 0 formal score change(s), 0 sub-threshold signal(s) across 0 entities.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: January 2026 Google settlement lifted ACC but exposed that pre-settlement EMP/SYS/INT scores were too generous given Garcia-case record.

item.title: New Yorker investigation alleging 'pattern of lying,' dissolved superalignment team, FL AG probe, whistleblower suppression, and DEI removal collectively warrant significant downgrade from Functional to Developing
